sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content:
Pathway: Locoregional Neuroendocrine Tumors of the GI Tract, Lung, and Thymus (Well-Differentiated Grade 1/2)
Branching basis: primary site (gastric, duodenal, appendiceal, jejunal/ileal/colon, rectal, lung, thymus), tumor size, tumor grade, lymph node status, disease stage, gastrin type (for gastric NETs), functional status

Criteria: Gastric NETs - Type 1 (hypergastrinemic, atrophic gastritis)
Options: For tumors ≤1 cm: surveillance or endoscopic resection (polypectomy or EMR for mucosal lesions). For tumors >1 but ≤2 cm: consider EUS, surveillance or endoscopic resection (EMR for mucosal; ESD or eFTR for submucosal). For tumors >2 cm: EUS, cross-sectional imaging, multidisciplinary discussion including surgical evaluation [NE-C 1 of 6, NET-1]

Criteria: Gastric NETs - Type 2 (hypergastrinemic, Zollinger-Ellison)
Options: Endoscopic resection of gastric tumors >1 cm and multidisciplinary approach to treat the underlying gastrinoma (see PanNET-3 for identification and treatment of gastrin-producing tumor) [NET-1]

Criteria: Gastric NETs - Type 3 (normal gastrin, sporadic)
Options: Partial or total gastrectomy (based on tumor location) with regional lymphadenectomy (preferred), or if no evidence of regional lymphadenopathy on EUS or other imaging, consider endoscopic resection or surgical wedge resection [NET-1]

Criteria: Duodenal NETs - non-functioning, non-ampullary, ≤1 cm
Options: For non-invasive tumors: endoscopic resection. For invasive tumors: local excision (transduodenal) with regional lymphadenectomy or pancreatoduodenectomy [NET-2]

Criteria: Appendiceal NETs - Tumor ≤2 cm, simple appendectomy performed
Options: For tumor <1 cm: no surveillance indicated. For tumors ≥1 to ≤2 cm: optional surveillance every 2–5 years based on clinicopathologic features [NET-3]

Criteria: Appendiceal NETs - Tumor >2 cm or any size with incomplete resection or positive lymph nodes
Options: Consider right hemicolectomy. Greater than 12 lymph nodes should be retrieved. For any tumor size with incomplete resection or positive lymph nodes within the appendectomy specimen, recommend complete staging imaging and proceed to right hemicolectomy [NET-3]

Criteria: Jejunal/ileal/colon NETs - Locoregional disease
Options: Bowel resection(s) with regional lymphadenectomy. Include manual palpation of entire bowel for synchronous tumors, assess proximity to superior mesenteric vessels, resection to root of mesentery [NET-4]

Criteria: Rectal NETs - Small (<1 cm), completely resected, incidental, negative margins, low grade
Options: Endoscopic surveillance [NET-5]

Criteria: Rectal NETs - T1 (≤2 cm)
Options: Resection (transanal or endoscopic excision, if possible). For 1- to 2-cm tumors, consider examination under anesthesia and/or EUS with radical resection if muscularis propria invasion or node positive [NET-5]

Criteria: Rectal NETs - T2–T4 or node positive, >2 cm
Options: Low anterior re...

flowcharts.title: Locoregional Neuroendocrine Tumors of the GI Tract, Lung, and Thymus (Well-Differentiated Grade 1/2)

nodes.label: Gastric NETs - Type 1 (hypergastrinemic, atrophic gastritis)

For tumors ≤1 cm: surveillance or endoscopic resection (polypectomy or EMR for mucosal lesions). For tumors >1 but ≤2 cm: consider EUS, surveillance or endoscopic resection (EMR for mucosal; ESD or eFTR for submucosal). For tumors >2 cm: EUS, cross-sectional imaging, multidisciplinary discussion including surgical evaluation [NE-C 1 of 6, NET-1]
Gastric NETs - Type 2 (hypergastrinemic, Zollinger-Ellison)
Endoscopic resection of gastric tumors >1 cm and multidisciplinary approach to treat the underlying gastrinoma (see PanNET-3 for identification and treatment of gastrin-producing tumor) [NET-1]
Gastric NETs - Type 3 (normal gastrin, sporadic)

Partial or total gastrectomy (based on tumor location) with regional lymphadenectomy (preferred), or if no evidence of regional lymphadenopathy on EUS or other imaging, consider endoscopic resection or surgical wedge resection [NET-1]
Duodenal NETs - non-functioning, non-ampullary, ≤1 cm
For non-invasive tumors: endoscopic resection. For invasive tumors: local excision (transduodenal) with regional lymphadenectomy or pancreatoduodenectomy [NET-2]
Appendiceal NETs - Tumor ≤2 cm, simple appendectomy performed
For tumor <1 cm: no surveillance indicated. For tumors ≥1 to ≤2 cm: optional surveillance every 2–5 years based on clinicopathologic features [NET-3]
Appendiceal NETs - Tumor >2 cm or any size with incomplete resection or positive lymph nodes

Consider right hemicolectomy. Greater than 12 lymph nodes should be retrieved. For any tumor size with incomplete resection or positive lymph nodes within the appendectomy specimen, recommend complete staging imaging and proceed to right hemicolectomy [NET-3]
Jejunal/ileal/colon NETs - Locoregional disease

Bowel resection(s) with regional lymphadenectomy. Include manual palpation of entire bowel for synchronous tumors, assess proximity to superior mesenteric vessels, resection to root of mesentery [NET-4]
Rectal NETs - Small (<1 cm), completely resected, incidental, negative margins, low grade
Endoscopic surveillance [NET-5]
Rectal NETs - T1 (≤2 cm)

Resection (transanal or endoscopic excision, if possible). For 1- to 2-cm tumors, consider examination under anesthesia and/or EUS with radical resection if muscularis propria invasion or node positive [NET-5]

edges.label: Partial or total gastrectomy with regional lymphadenectomy
